_comment: Canonical registry of HAMMER agent capabilities. Loaded into the decomposition prompt so Claude generates grounded configs. Update this file when new tools/hooks/MCP servers are added.

claude_code_tools._comment: Valid values for the allowed_tools array on each node. Only list tools the node actually needs.

hook_templates._comment: Named hook templates available via getDefaultHooks(). Leave hooks:{} to use type-based defaults (recommended). Reference template names here for documentation only — the server maps names to configs.

codeQuality.description: Auto-format code with prettier+eslint after every Write/Edit.
codeQuality.use_when: Leaf nodes writing production code (TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, etc.)

pathBoundary.description: Block writes outside the node's allowed_paths. Enforced via enforce_boundaries.py.
pathBoundary.use_when: ALL leaf and test nodes — prevents cross-boundary file corruption

typeCheck.description: Run tsc --noEmit after code changes. Surfaces TypeScript errors immediately.
typeCheck.use_when: TypeScript-heavy nodes that benefit from incremental type feedback

testRunner.description: Run jest --passWithNoTests --bail after every edit. Fast feedback loop.
testRunner.use_when: Test nodes and TDD-style leaf nodes

secretDetection.description: Scan for API keys, passwords, tokens before Write/Edit commits.
secretDetection.use_when: ALL leaf and test nodes — security guardrail

branchProtection.description: Block direct edits to main/master branch.
branchProtection.use_when: Nodes with git access that should never touch main

contractVerification.description: Run verify_contracts.py after edits — ensures interface contract compliance.
contractVerification.use_when: Leaf nodes that produce outputs consumed by sibling nodes

defaults_by_node_type._comment: When hooks:{} is left empty, the server applies these defaults automatically.

mcp_servers._comment: MCP servers available for the mcp_tools array. Include the full config object in mcp_tools.

context7.description: Fetch live, version-specific library documentation from the internet. Agents append 'use context7' to their prompts to activate.
context7.when_to_use: Any node working with third-party libraries (npm packages, pip packages, etc.) that may have changed APIs.

playwright.description: Full interactive Playwright browser control via MCP. High token cost (~13,700 baseline).
playwright.when_to_use: Tier 3 integration-test nodes ONLY. Never for routine debugging. HAMMER auto-enables Chrome DevTools MCP for browser failures instead.

browser_debugging_tools._comment: CLI browser tools available via Bash. HAMMER's tiered approach — always start with cheapest.

tier1_default.description: Default browser tool. Page loads, clicks, form fills, screenshots, DOM inspection.

tier1_fallback.description: CLI fallback when agent-browser insufficient. Supports network-idle wait, Firefox/WebKit.

tier2_auto.description: Auto-enabled by HAMMER on browser failures (CORS errors, blank pages, JS errors). Do NOT configure manually in node configs.
tier2_auto.usage: Automatic — HAMMER enables this when CLI tools fail

testing_tiers._comment: Set testing_tier on test/integration nodes. Determines which testing infrastructure runs.

tier1.label: CLI Tests
tier1.description: Lint (eslint), TypeScript type check (tsc), unit tests (jest). Zero agent tokens consumed. Runs synchronously.
tier1.assign_when: Default for ALL nodes. Pure code work, unit tests, no browser needed.

tier2.label: Screenshot Verification
tier2.description: Playwright CLI screenshot capture. Headless browser, no interactive tokens. Good for UI component validation.
tier2.assign_when: UI component nodes that need visual regression checking without full integration.

tier3.label: Integration Testing
tier3.description: Full Playwright MCP interactive browser testing. Runs AFTER all sibling nodes complete. Expensive but thorough.
tier3.assign_when: Integration-test nodes only (e.g. 'integration-tests', 'e2e-tests'). Never assign to feature nodes.

model_selection._comment: All decomposed nodes always use haiku. This is enforced server-side.
model_selection.sonnet: DO NOT USE — reserved for SCHEMA orchestration layer only
model_selection.opus: DO NOT USE — reserved for kingdom-wide architectural decisions only
